steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Command of Evidence (Quantitative/Textual). OFT says foraging behavior reflects cost-benefit trade-offs. The decisive clue is that higher lunar intensity is positively associated with bushbaby foraging specifically because their greater vision-reliance means the light benefits them more than it does woodrats.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — If increased lunar intensity imposed the same costs on the bushbabies that it imposes on white-throated woodrats, there would be no association between lunar intensity and the bushbabies' foraging.: the passage never specifies that lunar intensity imposes costs on woodrats — it says foraging is negatively associated, which under OFT could mean costs outweigh benefits, but the same cost structure need not produce zero association for bushbabies. Unsupported inference. ✗
  • Choice B — If the advantages that the bushbabies gain from increased lunar intensity explain the change in their foraging behavior, those advantages are likely shared by some other species that are not heavily reliant on vision.: the passage attributes bushbaby benefit specifically to their greater reliance on vision. Species not heavily reliant on vision would not share that same advantage. Contradicts the text’s logic. ✗
  • Choice C — If increased lunar intensity creates any disadvantages for the bushbabies, those disadvantages are more than compensated for by the advantages that the bushbabies gain.: under OFT, the net positive outcome (more foraging with more light) requires that benefits exceed costs. This is the only statement that must follow from the evidence. ✓
  • Choice D — If the bushbabies' foraging behavior under increased lunar intensity actually reflects a cost-benefit trade-off, their behavior should be more similar to that of white-throated woodrats than it is.: OFT explicitly allows different species to respond differently to the same circumstance based on their ecology — different responses are fully consistent with OFT, not a problem for it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
